name: CPT test from K0 - K0_bar mass difference
requirement: Any candidate framework must respect CPT symmetry to the precision set by the upper bound on the mass difference between the neutral kaon and its antiparticle: |m_{K^0} - m_{K^0_bar}| < 4.8 x 10^-19 GeV (95% CL, PDG, derived from CPLEAR + KLOE semileptonic-asymmetry analyses assuming CPT violation enters via the mass matrix only). This is one of the most stringent CPT tests in any system; expressed dimensionlessly, |Delta m / m_K| < 9.6 x 10^-19 (m_K = 0.4977 GeV).

regime.description: Neutral kaon system; CPT-violating mass splitting would induce specific CP-asymmetries in K_S - K_L oscillation.

current_bound.experiment: KLOE + CPLEAR analyses of CP-violating asymmetries in semileptonic K decays
current_bound.reference: Particle Data Group, 'Review of Particle Physics', Prog. Theor. Exp. Phys. 2024, 083C01 (2024); CPLEAR Collaboration, 'T-violation and CPT-tests in kaons', PLB 444, 52 (1998); KLOE Collaboration, 'Test of CPT and Lorentz symmetry in entangled neutral kaons with the KLOE experiment', PLB 730, 89 (2014), arXiv:1312.6818

what_it_excludes: Any framework that violates CPT (e.g., spontaneous CPT breaking in string theory, certain Lorentz-violating extensions) must keep the CPT-violating mass shift below 10^-19. Combined with antihydrogen 1S-2S, neutral B mass differences, and electron g-factor matching to positron, builds a sweeping cross-particle CPT test.

parameterization.name: SME meson-sector coefficients: (a_eff)^mu_q for each quark flavor q. Mass differences in neutral-meson systems probe specific combinations of SME coefficients depending on quark content.
parameterization.reference: Kostelecky and Potting, PRD 51, 3923 (1995), arXiv:hep-ph/9501341; Kostelecky and Russell, RMP 83, 11 (2011).
parameterization.summary: CPT violation in neutral kaons appears as a complex parameter delta_K in the K_S, K_L state mixing, with |delta_K| ~ |m_{K0} - m_{K0_bar}| / Gamma_S.

procedural.predicted_observable: Asymmetry parameter delta_K from semileptonic CP-asymmetries; equivalent to mass / width difference between K0 and K0_bar.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: Specific (a_eff)^0 quark coefficient combinations bounded at 10^-21 GeV level.
framework_inputs: Compute the framework's contribution to K0 - K0_bar mass-matrix difference, parameterized as CPT-violating SME coefficients.

primary_references.citation: Particle Data Group, 'Review of Particle Physics', Prog. Theor. Exp. Phys. 2024, 083C01 (2024)
CPLEAR Collaboration, Angelopoulos et al., 'T-violation and CPT-tests in neutral-kaon decays', PLB 444, 52 (1998)
KLOE Collaboration, Babusci et al., 'Test of CPT and Lorentz symmetry in entangled neutral kaons with the KLOE experiment', PLB 730, 89 (2014)
Kostelecky, 'Sensitivity of CPT tests with neutral mesons', PRD 64, 076001 (2001)
